channel.title: Retrieval on Charlie Hulcher
channel.description: Recent content in Retrieval on Charlie Hulcher

item.title: The Retrieval Paradox: Why Your Most Productive AI Tool Is Also Your Biggest Security Risk
item.description:
Every engineering team running AI agents is about to build the same thing. Some already have.

The pattern is obvious once you need it: connect to your corporate SaaS tools, extract the content, embed it, make it searchable. I built exactly this for my own agent infrastructure. Two open-source repos and a Mac Studio. Slack, Notion, Linear, Gmail, all synced locally, indexed with hybrid semantic search, queryable by any agent in the fleet.

item.title: Deep Retrieval: Organizational Understanding for AI Agents
item.description:
I built a retrieval system that lets my AI agents produce comprehensive reports across an entire organization’s history in about 7 minutes.

A 3-year-old company has thousands of Slack conversations, hundreds of Notion specs, years of Linear issues, and piles of email threads. Decisions get made in chat and forgotten. Context lives in one person’s head until they leave. Specs reference other specs that reference conversations that happened before half the team was hired.
